prompts: The law of universal gravitation, formulated by Isaac Newton, states that every point mass attracts every other point mass in the universe with a force that is directly proportional to the product of their masses and inversely proportional to the square of the distance between their centers. This force is expressed mathematically as F = G * (m1 * m2) / r², where F is the gravitational force, G is the gravitational constant, m1 and m2 are the masses involved, and r is the distance between the centers of the two masses.
